commands.name: bitmap_single
commands.description: Imports a single image file and converts it to a bitmap.

args.name: image-file
args.description: A local data path to a tif/tiff file with extension.

commands.name: bitmaps
commands.description: TIFF (.tif/.tiff) images can be compiled into a bitmap using the bitmaps verb:

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to a folder containing a set of images for processing.

commands.name: bitmaps-debug
commands.description: This command compiles images like the bitmaps command but was intended to debug the generated plate data. Obsolete as of H3.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to a folder containing a set of images for processing.

commands.name: bitmaps-with-type
commands.description: This command compiles images like the bitmaps command but allows the user to set the type of bitmap to generate.

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to a folder containing a set of images for processing.

args.name: type
args.description: sets the type of bitmap the image will be converted to. This will change how the source image is expected to be setup. The list of valid options is as follows:2d

commands.name: export-bitmap-dds
commands.description: Takes the processed pixel data from a bitmap tag and dumps it to a DDS image.

args.name: bitmap-tag
args.description: A local tag path to a bitmap tag without extension.

args.name: output-path-prefix
args.description: An absolute file path to a directory to output the DDS file to along with a prefix to append to the start of the DDS filename. If only a prefix is given then the file will be dumped at the root of your H3EK directory.

commands.name: export-bitmap-pfm
commands.description: Takes the processed pixel data from a bitmap tag and dumps it to a PFM image.

args.name: bitmap-tag
args.description: A local tag path to a bitmap tag without extension.

args.name: output-path-prefix
args.description: An absolute file path to a directory to output the PFM file to along with a prefix to append to the start of the PFM filename. If only a prefix is given then the file will be dumped at the root of your H3EK directory.

commands.name: export-bitmap-tga
commands.description: Takes the processed pixel data from a bitmap tag and dumps it to a TGA image.

args.name: bitmap-tag
args.description: A local tag path to a bitmap tag without extension.

args.name: output-path-prefix
args.description: An absolute file path to a directory to output the TGA file to along with a prefix to append to the start of the TGA filename. If only a prefix is given then the file will be dumped at the root of your H3EK directory.

commands.name: export-dxt5-array-from-bitmap-array
commands.description: Takes the processed pixel data from a bitmap tag and dumps it to PFM images.

args.name: bitmap-tag
args.description: A local tag path to a lightmap bitmap tag without extension.

args.name: output-path-prefix
args.description: An absolute file path to a directory to output the file to along with a prefix to append to the start of the filename. If only a prefix is given then the file will be dumped at the root of your H3EK directory.

commands.name: camera-track
commands.description: This commands takes a JMA file directly and converts it to a camera_track for the game. The length of the animation should be at most 16 as that is the max number of control points a camera track tag can contain. The JMA should contain a skeleton made up of a single bone that will change its position each frame. The orientation of the bone will determine the position and rotation of the control point for that index. The name of the JMA file will be the name of the tag. Keep in mind that the extension check for this command is case sensitive. Keep the JMA portion lower case.

args.name: source-file
args.description: A local data path to where the JMA file is located.

commands.name: collision
commands.description: A JMS file containing a collision model can be compiled into a collision model tag

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local data path to the root of a model source directory.

commands.description: Sets the render_model tag reference in a decorator-set tag.

args.name: render-model
args.description: A local tag path to a render_model tag file without extension.

commands.name: extract-import-info
commands.description: Dumps the stored import-info data used to originally import the tag file. Only tags that contain valid import-info can use this. This means only render_model, collision_model, physics_model, and scenario_structure_bsp.

args.name: tag-file
args.description: An absolute file path to a tag containing valid import info.

commands.name: extract-render-data
commands.description: Dumps the stored import-info data used to originally import the render_model file. Only render_model tags that contain valid import-info can use this.

args.name: render-model
args.description: A local tag path to a render_model tag without extension.

commands.name: fbx-to-ass
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to an ASS file for Halo 3 level importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: ass
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jma
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to an animation source file for Halo 3 importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script. Be aware that the extension can be any of the available extensions for animation importing. It does not specifically needs to be JMA. You can type JMO as the extension and the output is still valid.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jma
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jmi
commands.description: This command takes an FBX and converts it to a JMI source file for Halo 3 importing. Use this if you don't have access to an export script.

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jmi
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: fbx-to-jms
commands.description: FBX to JMS

args.name: render,collision,physics-or-all
args.description: Sets the type of geo this FBX is for. Use either render, collision, physics, or all only. The geo class set will determine what geometry gets exported from the JMS

args.name: fbx
args.description: An absolute filepath to a valid FBX file.

args.name: jms
args.description: An absolute filepath that includes name and extension to write the output to.

commands.name: reimport-sounds
commands.description: Goes through all sound tags in a directory or child directories. Any sound tags that are found will have their sound files from data reimported.
commands.example: tool reimport-sounds "sound\dialog\combat" default downmix unit_dialog english

args.name: source-directory
args.description: A local tag path to a directory or child directory containing sound tags.

args.name: compressor
args.description: Specify the compressor (default, pcm, xma, xma2)

args.name: downmix
args.description: Down mix the adpcm sample (downmix). This is an optional argument

args.name: bank:suffix-where-suffix-is-a-fmod-bank-suffix
args.description: Specify the fmod soundbank that should contain this sound. This is an optional argument

commands.name: reimport-sounds-single
commands.description: Reimports the specified sound if sound data was found in the data folder.
commands.example: tool reimport-sounds "sound\dialog\combat" default downmix unit_dialog english

args.name: source-file
args.description: A local tag path to a sound tag without extension.

args.name: compressor
args.description: Specify the compressor (default, pcm, xma, xma2)

args.name: downmix
args.description: Down mix the adpcm sample (downmix). This is an optional argument

args.name: bank:suffix-where-suffix-is-a-fmod-bank-suffix
args.description: Specify the fmod soundbank that should contain this sound. This is an optional argument

commands.name: reimport-sounds-for-senario
commands.description: Reimports the sounds for a scenario if the sound data is found in the data folder.
commands.example: tool reimport-sounds-for-senario "levels\multi\riverworld\riverworld" default downmix music sfx

args.name: scenario
args.description: A local tag path to a scenario without extension.

args.name: compressor
args.description: Specify the compressor (default, pcm, xma, xma2)

args.name: downmix
args.description: Down mix the adpcm sample (downmix). This is an optional argument

args.name: type
args.description: Only reimport the sample if it matches the specified type criteria.

args.name: bank:suffix-where-suffix-is-a-fmod-bank-suffix
args.description: Specify the fmod soundbank that should contain this sound. This is an optional argument

args.name: platform
args.description: The platform this cache file is being built for. If nothing is set then it will default to pc. This is an optional argument

args.name: dedicated-server
args.description: ??? This is an optional argument

args.name: use-fmod-data
args.description: ??? This is an optional argument

args.name: map-name
args.description: The filename of a map file located in your maps folder without extension

args.description: A local tag path to a scenario tag without extension.

commands.name: check-all-tags
commands.description: Checks if your tags are valid.

commands.name: convert-tag-files-explicit-pilot
commands.description: Converts tags from one backend to another. E.g. loose tag files to the monolithic backend. Seems to crash, maybe will be fixed in the future. Likely not useful for non-devs.

args.name: source-backend
args.description: Argument documented by the command syntax.

args.name: dest-backend
args.description: Argument documented by the command syntax.

args.name: optional:-tag-list
args.description: Argument documented by the command syntax.

args.name: input-tiff-file
args.description: An absolute path to a tiff file.

args.name: output-tiff-file
args.description: An absolute path to a path with filename and extension to generate the output file in.

commands.name: dialogue-globals-import
commands.description: Compiles a CSV file to generate a new ai_dialogue_globals tag. The file path for the CSV file is data\ai\ai_dialogue_globals.csv and tags\ai\ai_dialogue_globals.ai_dialogue_globals is generated.

commands.name: dump-render-method-options
commands.description: Dumps an index of all shader templates used by shaders, once it's done it prints out some statistics and saves information to the shaders folder in binary or text form. Required for building templates.

commands.name: export-shipping-game-variants
commands.description: Writes gametype bin files to your H3EK root. Currently broken.
